policy.repo_storage_rule: Store source URLs, license notes, extracted descriptors, and generated synthetic examples. Do not store third-party template PPTX files, screenshots, logos, private data, or distinctive copied geometry.
policy.release_rule: Generated gallery decks may be published when they use generic content and this manifest records the inspiration route and restrictions.

source_classes.license_summary: NASA content is generally not subject to U.S. copyright, but identifiers, endorsement, third-party materials, people or likenesses, and AI attribution restrictions can apply.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live NASA media guidance supports metadata-only or synthetic inspiration: NASA media are generally usable for educational or informational purposes, but NASA identifiers are protected, third-party materials may be copyrighted, commercial use must avoid endorsement, identifiable people may require permission, and AI outputs should not be attributed to NASA.

allowed_extractions: palette or contrast observations without identifiers
generic_style_observations: technical imagery works best when the figure owns the slide and labels stay small, factual, and source-backed
mission-style dashboards should use state, threshold, and next-action readouts rather than decorative chrome
scientific captions need enough room to explain context, limitations, and data provenance
generic_slide_patterns: large figure left or center with caption/source strip and interpretation sidecar
status console with one active route, two thresholds, and a next-action footer
map/field/technical panel followed by policy or engineering implication table
design_constraints: remove NASA identifiers and distinctive mission marks
replace direct media with generated or user-provided assets unless a deck-specific license chain is recorded
source_classes.attribution_policy: When direct NASA material is used in a deck, cite the source URL and avoid endorsement language. For this skill repo, prefer metadata-only notes or synthetic reconstructions.
source_classes.storage_policy: Do not bundle NASA source assets in the skill repo.

source_classes.license_summary: CDC agency materials are generally public domain unless otherwise noted, but contractor, grantee, state or local, third-party, logo, and endorsement restrictions can apply.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live CDC agency-material guidance supports public-domain use with attribution and disclaimer requirements, while explicitly warning that contractor, grantee, third-party, state/local, international, image, logo, and endorsement restrictions may apply.

allowed_extractions: public health source and disclaimer posture
clinical or lab report evidence hierarchy
small reference and attribution table patterns
generic_style_observations: public-health pages benefit from a plain-language finding before dense evidence details
clinical and lab reports need visible denominator, sample, method, caveat, and source fields
reference material should compress into source IDs on content slides and expand into editable tables near the end
generic_slide_patterns: finding headline with clinical implication sidebar and source/disclaimer line
protocol or assay table with semantic pass/review/fail cells and compact method metadata
references table with source ID, title, date/accessed field, and usage note
design_constraints: remove agency marks and partner logos
use synthetic cohorts, samples, and public-health examples unless real source rights are explicit
forbidden_materials: state or local partner material without rights
source_classes.attribution_policy: Attribute CDC material and include disclaimer language when a deck uses it directly. For this skill repo, store only high-level descriptors and generic examples.
source_classes.storage_policy: Do not bundle CDC or partner source decks, screenshots, or marks in the skill repo.

source_classes.license_summary: Accessibility guidance for presentation structure, reading order, contrast, slide titles, tables, and media alternatives. Treat as design and QA guidance, not a visual template asset.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live Section508.gov presentation guidance points to PowerPoint authoring/testing resources for accessible presentations. Treat this as constraints for structure, reading order, template hygiene, alternative text, and QA rather than as a visual template source.

generic_style_observations: every slide needs an explicit title, predictable reading order, and enough contrast before style polish matters
tables and charts should use direct labels, short legends, and redundant semantic cues instead of color-only meaning
decorative variation should never break the repeated structure that makes a deck scannable and testable
generic_slide_patterns: descriptive title, one evidence object, concise takeaway, and source line
chart with direct labels and adjacent data note rather than a tiny legend
table with real headers, no merged-cell dependency, and concise row labels
design_constraints: treat accessibility guidance as a hard QA constraint
do not use inaccessible screenshots of tables or charts when editable text is possible
forbidden_materials: using guidance as a copied visual template
dropping accessibility constraints during synthetic reconstruction
source_classes.attribution_policy: Reference Section508.gov in notes when deck-specific accessibility choices are derived from the guidance.
source_classes.storage_policy: Store only guidance descriptors and QA constraints.

source_classes.license_summary: Templates are offered under CC BY 4.0 with credit requirements and with care needed for bundled photographs, illustrations, icons, and other third-party credits.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live SlidesCarnival terms state templates are under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0, require appropriate credit, require keeping the credits slide when using included photographs, and prohibit redistributing template files as-is. Use only high-level descriptors or attributed direct use outside this repo.

allowed_extractions: non-distinctive structure ideas described in original words
synthetic recreation with generic content and attribution if direct use occurs
generic_style_observations: template galleries are useful for pacing archetypes, but direct geometry should be replaced with original grid systems
pitch and editorial decks need a stronger cover/body/closing rhythm than evidence-heavy reports
theme inspiration should become reusable constraints: cover archetype, content density, asset posture, and source handling
generic_slide_patterns: poster opener with one claim, one proof chip, and one supporting visual lane
before/after comparison with a verdict line instead of symmetric bullet lists
closing ask or recommendation table with proof, caveat, owner, and next milestone
design_constraints: do not bundle third-party templates or screenshots
if direct template use happens in a user deck, keep required credits outside this skill repo
forbidden_materials: bundled template PPTX files without attribution
third-party photos or icons without their own license chain
removing required credit from direct template use
source_classes.attribution_policy: If a deck directly uses SlidesCarnival templates or assets, preserve CC BY credit and asset credits. This skill repo should prefer synthetic descriptors.
source_classes.storage_policy: Do not bundle SlidesCarnival templates or screenshots in the skill repo.

source_classes.license_summary: USWDS is a U.S. government design system; treat government-authored design guidance as public-domain/CC0-style inspiration while checking third-party fonts, icons, examples, and project-specific assets separately.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live USWDS documentation provides public-sector design-system guidance for accessible components, layout, tokens, identifiers, and design principles. Use this as a public-domain design-system reference for generic layout constraints and navigation/data patterns, not as a bundled asset source.

allowed_extractions: policy and service-page structure without identifiers
generic_style_observations: policy and public-service decks read better with direct headings, visible status labels, and restrained civic color use
operational pages should privilege task state, owner, and next step over decorative storytelling
data tables need plain headers, consistent tokenized states, and enough row height for repeated use
generic_slide_patterns: policy decision slide with status label, short rationale, owner, and deadline
service dashboard with task state tiles above an exception table
public brief page with direct title, one evidence object, and a source/accessibility footer
design_constraints: remove U.S. government identifiers and agency-specific marks unless the user supplies rights/context
convert component inspiration into original slide geometry and editable PowerPoint shapes
forbidden_materials: third-party fonts or assets without their own license chain
source_classes.attribution_policy: Cite USWDS or the relevant government source when a deck explicitly uses its guidance. This repo stores only descriptors and synthetic reconstructions.
source_classes.storage_policy: Store URLs, descriptors, and generated examples only; do not bundle USWDS screenshots or third-party assets.

source_classes.license_summary: GOV.UK Design System guidance is suitable as Open Government Licence-style design inspiration, with government identifiers and third-party assets excluded from this repo.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live GOV.UK Design System documentation exposes public-service patterns for typography, spacing, components, forms, summaries, and task-oriented content. Treat it as generic guidance for clear editorial/policy structures and not as a source of bundled screenshots or official marks.

allowed_extractions: policy memo and public-service content rhythm
generic_style_observations: editorial and policy decks should use brutally clear headings, spare rules, and few decorative containers
summary-list structure is useful for evidence, caveat, owner, and date fields
forms and task pages translate into slide structures with one question, one answer, and one next action
generic_slide_patterns: policy summary slide with finding, evidence, caveat, and owner as aligned rows
minimal editorial page with one large statement and one compact source/readout block
case-study page with service moment, friction, intervention, and measured signal
design_constraints: remove GOV.UK crown, wordmarks, and exact page styling
translate component ideas into original slide layouts with generic labels and content
source_classes.attribution_policy: When deck-specific content uses GOV.UK guidance directly, cite the guidance URL. This skill repo keeps only generic descriptors and synthetic examples.
source_classes.storage_policy: Do not store GOV.UK screenshots, official marks, or copied component geometry in the skill repo.

source_classes.license_summary: Carbon and Carbon Charts provide open-source design and data-visualization guidance. Use only high-level chart, spacing, theme, and annotation descriptors unless a deck records a specific license chain.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live Carbon data-visualization documentation provides chart-type, theme, palette, interaction, labeling, and accessibility guidance for data-heavy interfaces. Treat it as open-source design-system inspiration for generic chart/table/dashboard patterns, not as bundled UI screenshots.

generic_style_observations: data-heavy slides need chart title, axis clarity, direct annotation, and a nearby decision readout
dark technical dashboards work when color marks state and thresholds, not decoration
board and ops decks should pair charts with exception or owner tables to preserve actionability
generic_slide_patterns: chart with right-side facts rail for target, variance, owner, and data cut
dashboard with metric strip, central trend, exception table, and decision footer
dark console page with threshold chart, active status rail, and escalation owner
design_constraints: do not bundle Carbon screenshots or UI kit files
translate data-vis guidance into editable PowerPoint charts/tables and original synthetic examples
forbidden_materials: Carbon or IBM marks without rights
third-party icons or UI kit assets
source_classes.attribution_policy: Cite Carbon documentation when a deck explicitly relies on its data-visualization guidance. This repo stores only generic descriptors.
source_classes.storage_policy: Store source URLs and descriptors only; do not bundle Carbon source assets, screenshots, or examples.

source_classes.source_name: CFPB Design System Data Visualization Guidelines
source_classes.license_summary: CFPB Design System documentation is an open public design-system reference; use as chart/accessibility guidance only, not as bundled screenshots, data, UI assets, or brand material.

source_verification.evidence_summary: Live CFPB data-visualization guidance requires a chart title, axis labels, downloadable source data, and alt text; it also describes optional figure numbers, intro text, data source, notes, supporting text, color, pattern, and accessibility guidance. Treat this as generic chart and source-data discipline for synthetic slide examples.

allowed_extractions: chart title and axis-label completeness rules
pattern and color redundancy for non-color-only meaning
generic_style_observations: chart slides need a descriptive title, labeled axes, and a nearby source/data-cut cue before visual styling
data-heavy decks should bind every chart to a CSV or Excel-like source artifact for reproducible rebuilds
color should be reinforced with pattern, label, or direct annotation so meaning is not color-only
generic_slide_patterns: chart plus compact source-data note, direct takeaway, and optional figure number
comparison chart with patterned series and sidecar caveat instead of a tiny legend
source-backed data slide that pairs a chart with a downloadable-data or artifact alias note
design_constraints: treat the guidance as chart and accessibility rules, not a visual template
translate requirements into editable PowerPoint charts, tables, and source-footers
source_classes.attribution_policy: Cite CFPB Design System guidance when a deck explicitly relies on its data-visualization rules. This skill repo stores only URLs, descriptors, and synthetic reconstructions.
source_classes.storage_policy: Store source URLs and descriptors only; do not bundle CFPB screenshots, data files, brand assets, or copied examples.

use_cases: lab report source and disclaimer posture
technical command-center cues without copied screens
investor memo pacing without copied templates
photo or artifact sidebar without copied assets
